title: ogiera ruderalis
intro: Ogiera ruderalis, also known as the "wild sage" or "Russian sage", is a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its silvery-gray foliage and small purple flowers. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its essential oil composition. The health benefits of Ogiera ruderalis include its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, improve respiratory health, and support the immune system. Ogiera ruderalis can be prepared as a tea, infused oil, or dried herb for use in traditional medicine, although it's worth noting that modern research on the plant is limited.
